title: Add to a conversation on an item using a QR code
excerpt:
I’m wanting to use a webform (that can accessed by scanning a QR code) to write an update on an item. 
I am building an Asset Maintenance board where I want to be able to have a QR code on each asset that anyone can scan…

posts.author: djacks (Dave)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m wanting to use a webform (that can accessed by scanning a QR code) to write an update on an item.

I am building an Asset Maintenance board where I want to be able to have a QR code on each asset that anyone can scan and add comment and image to an item on the board.

I can use the “write updates via email” function to enable users outside of the account to add updates, however i would like to use a simple form to submit the data rather than email.

Any ideas?

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
@djacks Hi. I’m happy to announce that Triggerly our app for generating QR codes on monday.com is availble. Check it out on https://monday.com/marketplace/10000413

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s a great idea — using QR codes for quick updates sounds super efficient for asset tracking. While the email-to-update method works, I agree that a clean webform would be more user-friendly, especially for people not on the platform.

One option could be using a third-party form tool like Typeform or Google Forms with Make (Integromat) or Zapier to send the submission as an update to the item via Monday’s API. Each QR code could link to a form pre-filled with the relevant item ID.

Would love to hear if anyone has already implemented something like this — seems like a solid use case!

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml:
I submitted my app called OnlyForm for monday review, it has that feature

so you can create a from that’s connected your board
but you can also use it update item or subitem
you can also add GA4 tag to track your form and indeed you can create QR code.

let me know if you need the access now I’ll DM you the link
